name: Roast Pork Loin Wtih Pinot Noir Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve 1/3 cup salt and 1/3 cup sugar in 2 1/2 quarts of warm water.
2. Add the pork loin, bay leaves, thyme leaves, coriander seeds, juniper berries, and 1/4 cup olive oil to the mixture. Marinate overnight in the refrigerator.
3. Remove the pork from the liquid and rub with olive oil and chopped thyme.
4. Preheat the oven to 325°F.
5. Heat a large saute pan and brown the pork.
6. Transfer the pork to the oven and roast for 30-40 minutes, or until the internal temperature reaches 155°F. Remove from heat and cover the roast.
7. Pour off any fat from the pan.
8. To make the sauce, add 2 cups of chicken broth to the pan and reduce by half. Add 1 cup of pinot noir and reduce again. Mix in the mustard.
9. Slice the pork and serve with the sauce.
